fact: November 17 is exactly 321 days into the year - that's like counting backwards from 44 days left until New Year's!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely nuts burying their winter stash - they can bury up to 25 nuts per hour!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're barely done with homework!
November 17 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign represented by a creature that glows under blacklight!
This is peak leaf-peeping time in the southern United States, when trees put on their final colorful show before going bald for winter!
Arctic foxes are typically busy growing their thick white winter coats right now, making them look like fluffy snowballs with legs!
The Leonid meteor shower peaks around this time every November, shooting up to 15 'falling stars' per hour across the night sky!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 17 means spring is almost over and summer heat is cranking up!
Many deciduous trees have officially gone dormant by this date, basically taking a months-long nap until spring wakes them up!
The moon phase around November 17 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the 'Frost Moon' - spooky!
This date sits exactly 35 days after the autumn equinox, meaning we're deep into the season when nights gobble up more time than days!
Woolly bear caterpillars are hunting for the perfect hiding spots to survive winter - their fuzzy bands supposedly predict how harsh it'll be!
